text: The surgeon locates the plane between the prostate and the neurovascular bundles, carefully preserving the nerves to maintain their function.
The surgeon carefully peels the neurovascular bundles from the surrounding tissue layer, avoiding thermal injury by using clips near the nerves. Blood vessels are cauterized as they are encountered.
The surgical assistant hands the surgeon a clipper, which is then used to apply a clip to a specific structure in the surgical field.
The surgeon clips the blood vessels while carefully identifying the nerve, ensuring it remains undisturbed during the procedure.
